title: Linear – The system for product development
metaDesc: Purpose-built for planning and building products with AI agents.
metaOgTitle: Linear – The system for product development
metaOgDesc: Purpose-built for planning and building products with AI agents.
heroH1:
The product development
system for teams and agents
heroSubhead: Render UI before vehicle_state sync when minimum required state is present, instead of blocking on full refresh during iOS startup.
heroSubheadNote: The heroSubhead above is the first visible 'issue' item in the animated hero UI demo — the actual marketing subhead is the H2: 'A new species of product tool. Purpose-built for modern teams with AI workflows at its core, Linear sets a new standard for planning and building products.'
heroMarketingSubhead: A new species of product tool. Purpose-built for modern teams with AI workflows at its core, Linear sets a new standard for planning and building products.

allHeadings.text:
The product development
system for teams and agents
A new species of product tool. Purpose-built for modern teams with AI workflows at its core, Linear sets a new standard for planning and building products.
Move work forward across teams and agents
Built for the future. Available today.

paletteNotes: Near-black background rgb(8,9,10) with near-white text rgb(247,248,248). Secondary/muted text rgb(138,143,152). Dark theme throughout — no white backgrounds.

fonts.fontFamily: "Inter Variable", "SF Pro Display", -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", Roboto, Oxygen, Ubuntu, Cantarell, "Open Sans", "Helvetica Neue", sans-serif
"Inter Variable", "SF Pro Display", -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", Roboto, Oxygen, Ubuntu, Cantarell, "Open Sans", "Helvetica Neue", sans-serif
"Inter Variable", "SF Pro Display", -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", Roboto, Oxygen, Ubuntu, Cantarell, "Open Sans", "Helvetica Neue", sans-serif

sections.gist: (empty — likely spacer or full-bleed hero media)
(empty — likely animated hero app demo UI)

paras: Turn conversations and customer feedback into actionable issues that are routed, labeled, and prioritized for the right team.
sections.label: Intake→
sections.gist: Intake workflow UI showing backlog, ENG issues, customer requests triage

paras: Plan and navigate from idea to launch. Align your team with product initiatives, strategic roadmaps, and clear, up-to-date PRDs.
sections.label: Plan→
sections.gist: Roadmap timeline spanning FEB-SEP with multiple initiatives (UI Refresh, Autonomy status clarity, etc.)

sections.firstHeading: Move work forward across teams and agents
paras: Build and deploy AI agents that work alongside your team. Work on complex tasks together or delegate entire issues end-to-end.
sections.label: Build→
sections.gist: AI agent (Codex) terminal output, agent working in kinetic-iOS repo

paras: Understand code changes at a glance with structural diffs for human and agent output. Review, discuss, and merge — all within Linear.
sections.label: Diffs→
sections.gist: Structural code diff for kinetic-ios HomeScreen.tsx showing React Native code changes

paras: Take the guesswork out of product development with project updates, analytics, and dashboards that surface what needs your attention.
iOS implementation is mostly complete, but Android updates are still work in progress
Risk of timeline slip if remaining design decisions aren't finalized soon
sections.label: Monitor→
sections.gist: Issue count chart, cycle time by agent (Cursor/Codex/No Agent), Weekly Pulse AI summary
Social proof / testimonials + '33,000 product teams' stat + customer stories CTA

sections.firstHeading: Built for the future. Available today.
sections.gist: Final CTA section — Get started + Contact sales

testimonials.quote: You'll probably build a better product, just because of the craft that using Linear infuses on your brain.
testimonials.attribution: Gabriel Peal, Staff Software Engineer, OpenAI

testimonials.quote: Our speed is intense and Linear helps us be action biased.
testimonials.attribution: Nik Koblov, Head of Engineering, Ramp

testimonials.quote: Linear is excellent, just excellent. It has the right opinions for fast moving teams.

socialProof.stat: Linear powers over 33,000 product teams. From ambitious startups to major enterprises.

imagery.notes: Hero uses animated React UI components (not video/canvas). Heavy SVG use (183 elements) for icons and illustrations. 32 img tags for logos and avatars.

animLib: Framer Motion (motion package) — confirmed via bundled chunks: MotionConfigContext, AnimatePresence, motion, use-spring, use-in-view, use-transform, use-motion-value, use-reduced-motion, ScrollAnimation, animate. Custom scroll-triggered section animations. No GSAP, Lenis, THREE.js, or Lottie detected.
